access_mechanism_type: formal_procurement (PAMI direct centralized purchasing for retirees/pensioners with 100% oncology coverage); supplementary judicial (amparo de salud) for non-formulary drugs and coverage denials; informal out-of-pocket co-payment when listed drugs become unavailable due to PAMI-pharmacy debt arrears
procurement_channels: PAMI centralized procurement via tenders (licitaciones publicas) for oncology drugs; framework agreement (Acuerdo de Medicamentos 2023) negotiated jointly with CILFA, CAEME and pharmacy chambers (COFA, FACAF, FAFAR); dispensing through ~13,000 affiliated retail pharmacies; high-cost cancer drugs (trastuzumab, palbociclib, immunotherapy) dispensed through PAMI special program. Approximately 5 million PAMI affiliates depend on this channel for cancer treatment.
key_actors: INSSJP/PAMI (Instituto Nacional de Servicios Sociales para Jubilados y Pensionados) - budget authority and centralized purchaser; PAMI Direccion Ejecutiva - signs procurement resolutions; CILFA and CAEME (industry chambers) - laboratory counterparties; COFA (Confederacion Farmaceutica Argentina), FACAF, FAFAR - pharmacy distribution networks; Ministry of Health of the Nation - oversight; Ministry of Economy - budget transfer authority; Tribunal de Cuentas / Auditoria General de la Nacion - audit oversight (denounced 2025 oncology overpricing case)
formulary_coverage: PAMI historically provided 100% coverage for oncology, HIV, hemophilia, hepatitis B/C drugs. Vivir Mejor program originally covered ~170 active ingredients/3,000 products free; Resolution 2431/24 (signed by Esteban Leguizamo, Aug 2024) reduced this to 109 active ingredients / ~2,000 products. Oncology medicines preserved at 100% coverage. Cisplatin, doxorubicin, carboplatin and trastuzumab fall within PAMI oncology benefit; trastuzumab biosimilar use is permitted. As of Nov 2024, 123 medications retained 100% coverage; 44 medications lost full coverage in Aug 2024 cut. [uncertain: exact count of oncology-specific molecules on 2025 formulary]
judicial_access_volume: PAMI affiliates use amparo de salud when coverage is denied or delayed. National-level study (Romero et al., 2017-2020) found 405 amparo cases for high-priced drugs across federal databases; oncology drugs (palbociclib 5.9%, agalsidase 4.7%) among top litigated; mean time from amparo filing to drug delivery 150 days. PAMI-specific annual amparo volume not publicly disaggregated. [uncertain: PAMI-specific annual amparo case count]
registration_timeline_generic: Not directly applicable to PAMI (regulatory function belongs to ANMAT). PAMI procures only ANMAT-registered products.
registration_timeline_biologic: Not directly applicable to PAMI (regulatory function belongs to ANMAT).
registration_authority: ANMAT (Administracion Nacional de Medicamentos, Alimentos y Tecnologia Medica) - regulatory; PAMI uses ANMAT registry as eligibility filter.
biosimilar_pathway: PAMI is a biosimilar adopter - uses ANMAT-approved biosimilar trastuzumab, rituximab, bevacizumab to lower oncology cost. Specific PAMI biosimilar policy not publicly codified. [uncertain: PAMI biosimilar-specific tender share]
registration_backlog: Not applicable to PAMI directly (ANMAT function).
reform_status: enacted: Resolution 2431/2024 reducing Vivir Mejor scope (Aug 2024); enacted: Acuerdo de Medicamentos 2023 framework with CILFA/CAEME/pharmacies; in_litigation/proposed: 2025 Auditoria General de la Nacion denuncia regarding oncology overpricing (alleged 16x overpayment vs. tender benchmark). PAMI structurally affected by DNU 70/2023 obras-sociales free-choice rules though PAMI is not technically an obra social.
currency_mechanism: PAMI budget denominated in pesos; oncology drugs (especially imported biologics) priced in implicit USD. CEPO 2019-2023, SIRA Oct 2023, SEDI Dec 2023, SEDI eliminated Feb 2025 (RG 5651/2025), main cepo lifted Apr 14 2025 - all materially affected PAMI's purchasing power.
pharma_import_cost_impact: Severe. 2018 to 2022 Argentine public spending on high-cost medicines surged from ~AR$204 million to AR$4.5 billion (driven by inflation and FX). Dec 2023 54% peso devaluation under Milei collapsed PAMI peso budget vs. USD-priced biologics; 2024 inflation 178% YoY further eroded purchasing power. Argentina cancer drugs priced 123% above UK average for high-priced agents (ASCO Global Oncology 2018).
financial_mechanism: direct_budget (PAMI receives payroll contributions from active workers + Treasury transfers); pays 100% for oncology drugs to retirees; pharmacies front-load dispensing and reimburse PAMI on 30-60-90 day cycles; co-payment exists for non-oncology Vivir Mejor drugs after 2024 cuts but not for chemotherapy.
payer_debt_crisis: Documented and severe. As of late 2025, PAMI owes ~AR$400 billion to pharmacy network; payment delays of 45-60+ days; oncology drug debts and diaper debts unpaid; pharmacies threatened to refuse PAMI prescriptions. CILFA laboratories temporarily covered pharmacy receivables. 2019 PAMI debt was AR$19 billion with operational deficit AR$8.6 billion. Jan 2025 La Nacion / Infobae denuncia: PAMI paid up to 16x licitacion price for oncology drugs - alleged corruption / overpricing scandal.
health_budget_pct_gdp: Argentina health spending ~9.5% GDP (2022, total); PAMI ~1.0-1.2% GDP. [uncertain: PAMI exact share]
shortage_rate: Not centrally tracked for PAMI. Anecdotal: oncology treatments interrupted or unable to start due to debt-driven supply disruptions (2025). [uncertain: quantitative shortage rate]
shortage_causes: Structural: PAMI debt to pharmacies/laboratories; under-funding due to peso depreciation against USD-priced biologics; centralized procurement bottlenecks. Scenario: 2024 Vivir Mejor coverage cut shifted demand to amparo; 2024 50%+ devaluation collapsed peso budget; alleged tender corruption (16x overprice).
shortage_monitoring_system: PAMI internal monitoring only; no public real-time dashboard. ANMAT Resolution 754/2025 [uncertain - not confirmed in search] introduces mandatory MA-holder shortage notification, but PAMI as payer not the reporter.
oncology_specific_shortage_events: January 2025: Auditoria General / La Nacion expose alleging PAMI paid 16x tender price for oncology medicines; oncology supply tensions reported. Late 2025 (Nov-Dec 2025/Feb 2026): pharmacy threats to refuse PAMI prescriptions due to AR$400B debt - oncology specifically flagged as 'unpaid debt category'. [uncertain: specific cisplatin/doxorubicin stockout dates]
source_citation: 1) PAMI - Acuerdo de Medicamentos 2023, https://www.pami.org.ar/novedades/novedad/1068; 2) La Nacion (Jan 22 2025) - Denuncian que el PAMI pago medicamentos oncologicos hasta 16 veces mas, https://www.lanacion.com.ar/politica/denuncian-que-el-pami-pago-medicamentos-oncologicos-hasta-16-veces-mas-que-el-valor-de-las-compras-nid22012025/; 3) Infobae (Dec 2 2024) - El PAMI restringio el acceso a medicamentos gratis para los jubilados; 4) La Nacion (Aug 30 2024) - Nuevo recorte en el PAMI: 44 medicamentos pierden cobertura; 5) PAHO Pharmaceutical Country Profile - Argentina; 6) Romero et al., Judicializacion de medicamentos de alto precio en Argentina, Medicina (B Aires) 2024, https://www.scielo.org.ar/scielo.php?pid=S0025-76802024000500445; 7) Tiempo Argentino - PAMI dejo a millones de afiliados sin cobertura al 100%; 8) Wikipedia - PAMI; 9) Inflation and Access to Essential Drugs by Retirees (PMC11165379, 2024); 10) Politica Argentina (Nov 2025) - PAMI deuda con farmacias.
data_reliability: medium - mix of primary government resolutions (high reliability) and investigative news on debt/overpricing (medium). PAMI does not publish granular oncology procurement data publicly.
uncertain: exact count of oncology-specific molecules on 2025 formulary
